metadata.description: Chinese vocabulary for making plans, meetings, and communication

content.name: next_one
content.side_e: xià ge xīngqī wǒ gēn nǐ jiànmiàn.
content.side_f: Next week I'll meet with you.
content.side_g: 下个 is a demonstrative + measure word combo meaning 'next (one)' for time units or items in a sequence (下个星期, 下个月, 下个人). Note that 下个星期 is informal; 下星期 is also acceptable. It pairs with 上个 (shàng ge, 'last'). Tone note: 个 is toneless here.

content.name: below_next
content.side_g: 下 literally means 'below/down' but as a temporal prefix means 'next' (下周, 下次, 下午). It also functions as a directional complement (坐下 'sit down'). Distinguish from 下个 which requires a measure-word noun; bare 下 attaches directly to time words like 下周, 下次.

content.name: chinese_language
content.side_f: I must prepare for the Chinese exam.
content.side_g: 中文 refers to the Chinese language, especially written form or as a school subject. 汉语 (Hànyǔ) emphasizes spoken Mandarin as a linguistic system, while 普通话 (Pǔtōnghuà) is 'standard Mandarin.' In Taiwan 國語 is preferred. 中文 is the most neutral and common in everyday speech.

content.name: language_script
content.side_e: wǒ xiǎng duō xué yìxiē wénzì.
content.side_f: I want to learn more characters (lit. 'text-characters').
content.side_g: 文 is a bound morpheme meaning 'writing/language/script' (中文, 英文, 法文, 文字, 文化). Rarely stands alone in modern speech; almost always appears in compounds. Distinguish from 字 (zì, individual character) and 语 (yǔ, spoken language).

content.name: to_help
content.side_g: 帮 is the colloquial short form; 帮助 (bāngzhù) is the fuller, more formal verb/noun. Common pattern: 帮 + person + VP ('帮我练习' = help me practice). Also 帮忙 (bāng máng, VO: 'do a favor'). Do not say 帮助我练习 in casual speech — use 帮我练习.

content.name: to_prepare
content.side_g: 准备 is both verb ('to prepare') and noun ('preparation'). Common collocations: 准备考试, 准备饭, 准备好了 ('ready'). The resultative 准备好 is extremely common for 'be ready.' Register is neutral — works in both casual and formal contexts.

content.name: to_practice
content.side_e: wǒ měi tiān liànxí shuō Zhōngwén.
content.side_f: I practice speaking Chinese every day.
content.side_g: 练习 functions as both verb and noun ('to practice' / 'an exercise, drill'). Takes VP or NP objects: 练习说中文, 做练习. Slightly more formal than 练. Distinguish from 复习 (fùxí, 'review') and 学习 (xuéxí, 'study').

content.name: to_say_speak
content.side_e: tā shuō tā shuō de bù hǎo.
content.side_f: She says she doesn't speak well.
content.side_g: 说 covers both 'say' (reporting) and 'speak' (a language). For languages use 说 + 语言: 说中文, 说英文 — NOT 讲中文 in most mainland contexts (讲 is acceptable too, slightly more formal/'lecture'-like). The 得 complement pattern 说得好/不好 evaluates manner of speaking.

content.name: exclamation_particle
content.side_e: hǎo a! nǐ bāng wǒ ba.
content.side_g: 啊 is a sentence-final particle softening tone, expressing enthusiasm, mild surprise, or rhetorical emphasis. Tone shifts with preceding vowel (phonetic variants 呀/哇/哪). Distinguish from 吗 (yes/no question) and 吧 (suggestion/confirmation). Overuse sounds childish; one per clause max.

content.name: but
content.side_e: wǒ xiǎng qù, dànshì wǒ méiyǒu shíjiān.
content.side_f: I want to go, but I don't have time.
content.side_g: 但是 is the standard 'but' conjunction, slightly more formal than 可是 (kěshì). Often paired with 虽然 (suīrán...但是...) 'although...but...'. 不过 (búguò) is a softer, more conversational alternative. 但是 appears clause-initially, never mid-clause.

content.name: must_have_to
content.side_g: 得 (děi) is a colloquial modal meaning 'must/have to' (obligation), common in Beijing/northern speech. WARNING: this character has three readings — děi (must), de (structural particle 说得好), dé (obtain, 得到). Negative is 不用 (búyòng, 'don't need to'), NOT 不得. More formal equivalent: 必须 (bìxū).

content.name: with
content.side_f: I want to meet with you.
content.side_g: 跟 is a preposition 'with/and', used before verbs of interaction: 跟...说, 跟...见面, 跟...一起. Near-synonyms 和 (hé) and 与 (yǔ, formal) — 跟 is the most conversational/northern. Word order: Subject + 跟 + Object + Verb (跟你见面, NOT 见面跟你).

content.name: to_meet_up
content.side_a: to meet up; to meet with
content.side_g: 见面 is a separable verb-object compound (离合词): you cannot say 见面你 — must say 跟你见面 or 见你的面. Other separable compounds: 帮忙, 睡觉, 结婚. Also splits for aspect: 见过一次面. Means face-to-face meeting, not a virtual one (use 视频 for video call).

content.name: face
content.side_e: wǒmen jiàn yí cì miàn ba.
content.side_g: 面 alone means 'face/surface' and is a bound morpheme in most modern compounds (见面, 面子, 前面, 下面). Also the classifier for flat objects (一面镜子) and, with different etymology, 'noodles' (面条). Rarely stands alone for 'face' — use 脸 (liǎn) for the physical face in isolation.

content.name: to_come_back
content.side_g: 回来 is a directional complement compound: 回 (return) + 来 (come, toward speaker). Contrast 回去 (return away from speaker). Can split around an object: 回家来, 回学校去. Common pattern with time: 下个星期回来. The 来/去 is toneless in this compound.
